MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-25-2016 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FELIX MENJIVAR'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IN ITS ENTIRETY. THAT RECOMMENDATION IS: THE PHRASE "INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO" IS DELETED FROM THE DEFINITION OF THE TERM "TRANSMISSION DEFECT." WHERE FORD HAD AGREED TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS WITHOUT WAIVING ITS OBJECTIONS, FORD MUST SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT THAT IT HAS WITHHELD ON THE GROUNDS OF PRIVILEGE. NO PRODUCTION IS REQUIRED PRIOR TO THE ENTRY OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

APPEARANCES: Atty. NEIL GIELEGHEM, ESQ. of Law Offices of Neil Gieleghem, Tel No. 310-929-4900 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF FELIX MENJIVAR'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, the court's adopts the recommendation of the judge pro tem in its entirety. That recommendation is: The phrase "including but not limited to" is deleted from the definition of the term "Transmission Defect." Where Ford had agreed to produce documents without waiving its objections, Ford must specifically identify any responsive document that it has withheld on the grounds of privilege. No production is required prior to the entry of a protective order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
